General.details: Provides access to all VS Code commands, settings, and features through a searchable interface.
Allows you to search for and open any file in the current workspace by typing its name.
Opens the Settings editor to customize VS Code's behavior, appearance, and extension configurations.
Shows the Extensions view to browse, install, and manage VS Code extensions.
Shows or hides the primary sidebar (Explorer, Search, Source Control, etc.).
Shows or hides the bottom panel containing Terminal, Output, Problems, and Debug Console.
Closes the active file or editor tab.
Opens a side-by-side live preview of the current Markdown file.
Expands the VS Code window to fill the entire screen, hiding title bars and taskbars.

Editing.details: Adds the next instance of the currently selected text to the selection, enabling multi-cursor editing.
Editing.example: Select 'foo', then Ctrl+D to select next 'foo'

Editing.details: Adds all instances of the currently selected text to the selection for simultaneous editing.
Editing.example: Select 'bar', then Ctrl+Shift+L to edit all 'bar's

Editing.details: Inserts an additional cursor at the clicked location without losing existing cursors.
Applies the configured code formatter (e.g., Prettier, ESLint) to the active file.
Comments or uncomments the current line or selected lines using the language-specific comment syntax.
Wraps the selected text in a block comment (e.g., /* ... */ in JS).

Editing.desc: Move the current line up or down
Editing.details: Swaps the current line or selected lines with the line above or below it.

Editing.desc: Copy the current line up or down
Editing.details: Duplicates the current line or selected lines to the position above or below.
Manually invokes the IntelliSense completion list for the current context.
Renames the symbol under the cursor (variable, function, etc.) across the entire workspace.

Navigation.details: Prompts for a line number and jumps the cursor to that line in the current file.
Opens a quick-switch menu to navigate through recently used editor tabs.
Jumps to the source code where the symbol under the cursor is defined.
Opens a peek view showing all locations in the workspace where the symbol is used.
Opens a small inline window showing the definition of the symbol without leaving the current file.

Search.details: Opens the search widget for the active editor window.
Opens the replace widget for the active editor window.
Opens the Search view in the sidebar to search for text in all files in the workspace.
Opens the Replace view in the sidebar to perform text replacement in all workspace files.

Terminal.details: Shows or hides the built-in terminal at the bottom of the editor.
Creates an additional terminal instance in the bottom panel.
